title: Critical-device charging priority table
summary: Rank medical devices, emergency communication, lights, navigation, refrigeration monitoring, and comfort devices by life-safety effect and remaining runtime.
content: Rank medical devices, emergency communication, lights, navigation, refrigeration monitoring, and comfort devices by life-safety effect and remaining runtime. Assign charging windows and stop low-priority use before the reserve reaches the emergency floor. Do not connect an unknown device or damaged cable to the last trusted power source.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Critical-device charging priority table”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Critical-device charging priority table»
steps: Rank medical devices, emergency communication, lights, navigation, refrigeration monitoring, and comfort devices by life-safety effect and remaining runtime.
Assign charging windows and stop low-priority use before the reserve reaches the emergency floor.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Critical-device charging priority table.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Critical-device charging priority table.
warnings: Do not connect an unknown device or damaged cable to the last trusted power source.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Critical-device charging priority table.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Critical-device charging priority table.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Critical-device charging priority table.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Critical-device charging priority table.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Critical-device charging priority table.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Critical-device charging priority table.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Critical-device charging priority table.
short_term: For Critical-device charging priority table, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Critical-device charging priority table, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Critical-device charging priority table, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Critical-device charging priority table to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
